news.title: NCLT allows withdrawal of insolvency proceedings against ATS Knightsbridge
news.para: NCLT has permitted the withdrawal of insolvency proceedings against Noida's ATS Knightsbridge residential project after a settlement with lenders. The Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process, initiated in 2023, was formally closed on March 13, 2026. The project is nearing completion, with a tentative handover expected in 18-24 months.

news.title: Vedanta moves NCLAT, challenges NCLT nod to Adani's bid for acquiring Jaiprakash Associates
news.para: Vedanta Group has appealed to the NCLAT against the NCLT's decision. The NCLT had approved Adani Group's bid to acquire Jaiprakash Associates Ltd. Vedanta was also in the race to acquire the company. The Committee of Creditors had previously approved Adani's plan. Jaiprakash Associates has significant assets across real estate, cement, and hospitality.

news.title: Chandigarh housing board to hike restoration charges for resumed properties
news.para: Chandigarh Housing Board is set to significantly hike restoration charges for properties resumed due to dues or violations, potentially by ₹1 lakh to ₹3 lakh. This move aims to curb unauthorized construction and encourage timely payments. Meanwhile, the administration seeks approval to regularize GPA property transfers, expecting to recover substantial lost revenue from stamp duty.

news.title: Over 350 government offices haven't paid Nagpur civic body ₹145 crore in property tax
news.para: Nagpur Municipal Corporation is struggling to collect over 145 crore rupees in property tax from government departments. This is a major hurdle as the civic body aims to meet its revenue targets. Despite efforts and issuing notices, recovery from these departments remains slow. The corporation is considering a property tax hike for citizens to compensate for the shortfall.

news.title: Goa PCB issues show-cause notice to housing project in Vasco for discharging untreated sewage
news.para: Goa State Pollution Control Board has issued a show-cause notice to Prabhu Violetta Phase I residential complex in Vasco for discharging untreated sewage. The complex faces potential environmental compensation of Rs 44,50,000 and Rs 12,50,000 for violating pollution norms and operating without valid consent. The board ordered immediate cessation of discharge and daily sewage transport to a public facility.

news.title: Bhubaneswar: BMC issues stop-construction order to builder after residents' complaints
news.para: Bhubaneswar Municipal Corporation has halted construction at a Nandan Vihar housing project following resident complaints of unauthorized expansion and ORERA norm violations. Despite the builder's application rejection, work continued. Residents cited issues like unaddressed infrastructure and parking, prompting BMC's intervention and calls for stricter action against the developer.

news.title: Nashik: Revenue department issues revision notices to 439 people over alleged sub-division of their plots
news.para: Nashik authorities have issued revision notices to 439 individuals linked to 49 land deals, alleging fabricated sub-division maps were used to circumvent housing scheme regulations. Developers are accused of creating artificial land fragments under 4,000sqm to avoid reserving 20% for EWS/LIG housing, potentially costing Nashik 6,500 homes.

news.title: Jaipur development body expansion of no benefit to residential areas on outskirts
news.para: Residents in Jaipur's peripheral colonies face a struggle for basic civic amenities, with a recent sewerage tank issue highlighting the problem. The proposed expansion of the Jaipur Development Authority's jurisdiction under Master Plan 2047 is expected to worsen these difficulties by encouraging low-density growth in remote areas lacking adequate infrastructure.

news.title: VerbaFlo raises $7 million led by Pi Labs
news.para: VerbaFlo, a proptech startup, has secured $7 million in a seed funding round led by Pi Labs. The company, founded in October 2024, utilizes conversational AI to automate real estate operations and resident engagement. This funding will fuel VerbaFlo's expansion into the US and other international markets, alongside product development and team scaling.

news.title: Chandigarh sector 22-B building declared unsafe
news.para: A major fire in Sector 22-B's commercial building has rendered the top floor unsafe due to significant cracks and extensive structural damage. A short circuit is the preliminary cause, with nearly four hours and over 2 lakh litres of water used to control the blaze. A structural engineer's assessment is now crucial for the building's future.

news.title: Illegal sand mining racket busted in Thane, machinery of ₹90 lakh destroyed
news.para: Thane district authorities have launched a major crackdown on illegal sand mining in Bhiwandi taluka. The revenue department destroyed barges and suction units valued at approximately Rs 90 lakh. The operation targeted the Kevani creek area. Storage tanks for illegally extracted sand were also demolished.

news.title: Yamuna Expressway authority clears ₹11,829 crore budget, 67% for land acquisition
news.para: The Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority has approved a substantial budget for the upcoming financial year. A major focus is land acquisition for a new industrial and urban ecosystem near the Noida International Airport. Significant funds are also allocated for development in Yamuna City and airport-related infrastructure. Plot allotment rates will see a modest increase.

news.title: Kerala state housing board to reconstruct uninhabitable Chottanikkara flat
news.para: Kerala State Housing Board will reconstruct a faulty flat in Chottanikkara for Rs 2.27 crore after structural issues rendered it uninhabitable. The three-storeyed gypsum panel flat, built for 24 families, will be demolished and rebuilt with concrete. The decision follows a High Court directive after authorities failed to monitor construction, leading to families being relocated.

news.title: Jaipur's draft master plan 2047 lacks central government's guidelines: Experts
news.para: A new study reveals significant flaws in Jaipur's Master Plan 2047, suggesting its proposed expansion violates URDPFI guidelines. Experts warn the plan's inclusion of vast rural areas and excessive urbanizable land could severely impact civic services and infrastructure quality for residents.

news.title: ED attaches fresh ₹5,000 crore assets in case against PACL
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has attached properties valued at over Rs 5,000 crore. This action is part of a money laundering probe into the PACL ponzi scheme. The scheme allegedly defrauded lakhs of investors of approximately Rs 48,000 crore. This latest attachment brings the total seized assets to over Rs 22,000 crore. The investigation stems from a 2014 CBI case.

news.title: Delhi: Get provisional NOC for house by paying 25% of infrastructure charges upfront
news.para: Delhi Jal Board has approved a significant reform in infrastructure charge payments, allowing property owners to obtain a provisional NOC by paying 25% upfront during building plan approval. This move aims to unlock stalled projects and ease financial burdens, with the remaining charges adjusted later based on final rates.

news.title: Lucknow: LDA clears ₹5,148 crore budget with focus on affordable housing, new schemes
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority has approved a Rs 5,148 crore budget for 2026-27, prioritizing affordable housing and new townships. The plan includes Rs 200 crore for 848 flats in Sharda Nagar Extension and Rs 1,600 crore for land acquisition. The budget also allocates funds for infrastructure development, parks, and measures against illegal construction.

news.title: With 12,772 cases, Karnataka ranks fourth nationally in RERA complaints
news.para: Karnataka shows strong Rera activity with many complaints filed and resolved.  This indicates a healthy real estate market and active buyer engagement.  However, the actual execution of Rera orders, like getting money or keys, remains a significant hurdle.  District administration bottlenecks slow down the process.  Nationally, Uttar Pradesh leads in complaint filings.

news.title: Goldman Sachs, ICICI Pru MF buy DLF shares worth ₹231 crore via block deals
news.para: Goldman Sachs and ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund collectively invested Rs 231 crore in DLF through block deals, acquiring shares at Rs 531.7 apiece. This comes as DLF shares have underperformed, sliding 22% over the past year, mirroring the broader realty sector's downturn. Despite a dip in quarterly sales bookings, the company reported strong cash generation and achieved zero gross debt.

news.title: Paranjape Schemes to invest ₹700 crore to develop residential projects in Pune
news.para: Paranjape Schemes Construction is set to invest ₹700 crore in developing five residential towers across Pune's western corridor, specifically Sinhgad Road, Bhugaon, and Hinjewadi Phase 1. These projects, offering over 1.75 million sq ft and approximately 1,400 units, are projected to generate ₹1,175 crore in revenue. The company is also advancing three large integrated townships.
